metadata.title: AlphaVest Collaborative Workflow Manual V3
metadata.boundary: Manual-content source package only; no application behavior changes and no production-certification claim.

manualThesis: AlphaVest WealthOS is a collaborative workflow system for complex family-office and advisory operations. It does not simply store data. It turns fragmented inputs, documents, signals, recommendations, approvals, decisions, communications, and exports into a controlled shared state. Each role sees the right slice of the truth at the right time, and sensitive outcomes only become client-visible after the required human review, evidence, permission, and compliance gates.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Tenant, profile, entity, document, message, or request data
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Tenant setup, client profile, entity list, document upload, communication centre
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Internal teams see a scoped work item, data gap, or review-ready object inside the tenant context.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Advice-like conclusions, unreleased recommendations, restricted documents, and unapproved exports remain hidden.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Sensitive creates, uploads, invitations, and role changes should create audit lineage.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Missing tenant scope, missing consent, invalid invitation, unsupported document type, or missing required context.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Raw profile fields, entity details, extraction output, trigger context, or queue item
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Extraction review, workbench, signals, ops queues
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Reviewers see structured context instead of raw fragments: who owns it, what is missing, what is sensitive, and what action is next.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Client-facing advice, compliance release, and final evidence packages remain unavailable.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Corrections, classification, and scope decisions should be traceable when they affect later release or evidence.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Low-confidence extraction, conflicting relationship data, missing source document, or unclear owner.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Normalized signal, document, recommendation candidate, export scope, or governance request
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Workbench, advisor approval queue, compliance queue, export preview, governance access requests
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: The next role sees why the item is ready, what evidence supports it, and which gate still controls visibility.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Client-visible release stays blocked until compliance and permission conditions are satisfied.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Approvals, revisions, blocks, and requests for more data should create audit and evidence items.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Advisor approval missing, evidence incomplete, permission invalid, ROA/disclosure state unresolved.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Advice-like recommendation, sensitive access change, export package, or client-visible message
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Compliance release, compliance block, governance second confirmation, export redaction
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Approved material becomes available to the correct downstream role; blocked items return with a reason and next legitimate action.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Anything blocked, restricted, or redacted remains hidden from unauthorized roles.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Gate outcomes are evidence and audit events, not casual UI clicks.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Evidence incomplete, redaction missing, second confirmation missing, or client-visibility rule not satisfied.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Released recommendation, decision, evidence record, message, or export
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Decision room, evidence vault, portal, export download/share
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Clients see only released, scoped, or redacted material; internal teams see full lineage and control history.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Internal notes, unreleased drafts, raw extraction data, and restricted evidence remain internal.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Visibility changes must preserve who released what, why, and under which permission context.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Permission check fails, tenant scope mismatch, sensitivity too high, or release state absent.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Released decision object with evidence context
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Decision list, digital decision room, decision success, evidence record
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Advisor, compliance, and operations see the decision outcome, evidence link, and review cadence.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Unrelated internal review notes and restricted evidence remain hidden.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Decision action and reason should create immutable evidence and audit lineage.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Required participant has not acted, release missing, or evidence package incomplete.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Decision, compliance release, document, approval, communication, export, or audit event
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Evidence vault, evidence record detail, export scope/redaction, audit history
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Each role sees proof appropriate to its permission and visibility scope.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Highly restricted or internal-only evidence is hidden or redacted.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Evidence items preserve the relationship between source object, approval, decision, and audit trail.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Source object missing, redaction incomplete, or evidence visibility conflicts with role.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Sensitive action, state transition, release, block, export, access change, or denied action
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Audit history, compliance audit, evidence record, ops queues
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Control owners see lineage, result, actor role, target object, previous state, next state, and reason.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Audit details remain internal unless released as an approved evidence or export item.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Audit is append-only; corrections are new events, not edits.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: Missing actor, missing reason, invalid scope, or attempted cross-tenant action.

collaborationModel.incomingObject: Queue item, SLA breach, data quality issue, roadmap scope, or state reference
collaborationModel.uiSurface: Ops queues, SLA, service blueprint, roadmap, states
collaborationModel.whatOtherRolesSeeNext: Teams see where workflow friction accumulates and which service or product action should follow.
collaborationModel.notVisibleYet: Reference and operational pages remain internal-only.
collaborationModel.evidenceAuditImplication: Operational changes should be audited when they affect service commitments or client-visible workflow state.
collaborationModel.commonBlockedCondition: No owner, stale SLA, unresolved data quality issue, or implementation gap.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: The platform role establishes the rule set that later tenant, evidence, export, and release workflows inherit.
roleRelays.stateChange: Policy settings move from draft or review candidate into a reviewed baseline.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Compliance sees advice-boundary, evidence, release, and export rules that determine whether later work can become client-visible.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Treat policy review as client release
Bypass audit on sensitive policy changes
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Policy and role/security changes are expected to create audit events.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Indirect but foundational: later client visibility depends on this baseline.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Compliance confirms the policy meaning; security confirms access, session, and second-confirmation controls.
roleRelays.stateChange: Sensitive configuration remains blocked until confirmation and audit expectations are satisfied.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Security sees which policy areas affect permission, MFA/session, external access, and export behavior.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Make unrestricted client visibility the default
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Second-confirmation and security changes are audit-sensitive.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Prevents unsafe visibility defaults from being inherited by tenants.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Ops creates the tenant shell so a family office can be configured in a scoped environment.
roleRelays.stateChange: Tenant moves from draft to onboarding readiness.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Client Success sees owner assignments, missing activation gates, invitation readiness, and tenant policy profile.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Claim production onboarding persistence beyond demo source
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Tenant creation, team assignment, policy override, and invitation should be audited.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: No client-facing workspace is reliable until activation gates are satisfied.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Client Success sends the invitation only after tenant setup has enough owner, policy, and role context.
roleRelays.stateChange: User moves from invited toward active/demo role-confirmed state.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: The invited user sees identity, privacy acknowledgement, consent, and role confirmation surfaces.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Treat demo onboarding as production authentication
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Consent and onboarding audit are expected; real auth is intentionally deferred.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Creates scoped access context for later client-side visibility.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Client-side roles provide facts, relationships, preferences, and structure context that internal teams cannot safely infer.
roleRelays.stateChange: Profile and relationship data become reviewable tenant context.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: The analyst sees a structured family context with missing evidence, conflicts, and review needs.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Treat unverified client input as final evidence
Expose internal notes to client roles
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Profile and family edits should create audit events and review tasks when material.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Client input can remain visible to the client, but internal interpretation and advice remain gated.

roleRelays.objectOrState: Entity, document, signal, or recommendation candidate
roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Analysts normalize data chaos into structured, reviewable context before advisory judgement is applied.
roleRelays.stateChange: Workflow moves from analyst review toward advisor review or awaiting information.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Advisor sees the candidate package, source context, evidence status, and unresolved blockers.
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Analyst routing and advisor decision should create audit/evidence lineage.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Advisor approval is internal human review, not client visibility.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: The source document enters the system from the party that has it, but extracted values must be reviewed before they become trusted context.
roleRelays.stateChange: Document moves uploaded -> ai_extracted -> client_confirmed -> analyst_review_pending.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Analyst sees document type, sensitivity, extracted values, confidence, corrected values, and entity links.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Treat low-confidence extraction as accepted evidence
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Upload, extraction review, versioning, and evidence links should audit.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Clients may see simplified document state; internal extraction detail is not automatically client-visible.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Advisor review supplies human judgement and rationale, but the package still requires release control.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Compliance sees advisor rationale, evidence completeness, classification, permissions, and release/block controls.
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Compliance release/block must create audit and evidence lineage.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: This relay is the decisive client-visibility gate.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Compliance releases only the version that is safe, scoped, evidenced, and permitted for client review.
roleRelays.stateChange: Decision moves released_to_client -> awaiting_family_approval or decision-ready.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Client-side participants see the released decision, evidence summary, required participants, and accept/defer/reject controls.
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Decision action and reason should create evidence and audit lineage.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Client visibility is now permitted but still role/participant scoped.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: A user or admin requests changed access because collaboration requires the right people to see the right objects.
roleRelays.stateChange: Access request moves requested -> in_review -> approved/blocked/revoked.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Compliance/Security sees requester, target user, scope, sensitivity, reason, and second-confirmation need.
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Sensitive role/access changes require second confirmation and audit history.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Access changes alter who can see client-visible, restricted, or internal objects later.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: The internal team chooses the right channel when digital-only work is no longer enough.
roleRelays.stateChange: Communication moves draft/scheduled -> sent/completed/evidence-linked when material.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Client sees only the approved or appropriate message/call context, not internal notes.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Receive advice-like messages without release controls
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Sensitive communication should audit and may become evidence-linked.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Channel choice determines what is client-visible and what remains internal service context.

roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: The business wants to share evidence or decision material outside the live UI, but export creates risk.
roleRelays.stateChange: Export request moves draft -> scope_selected -> redaction_pending -> approval_required/approved -> generated/downloaded.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Privacy Officer sees export type, scope, redaction profile, external sharing context, expiry, and approval state.
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Export create, approval, download, and share should audit.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Exports create a controlled external visibility surface separate from the app UI.

roleRelays.objectOrState: Queue, SLA, roadmap, and state friction
roleRelays.whyFirstRoleActs: Ops observes bottlenecks and service friction that reveal where the workflow model needs improvement.
roleRelays.stateChange: Queue/data quality issue moves open -> in_review/blocked/resolved/deferred.
roleRelays.whatNextRoleSees: Product and QA see which states, queues, or routes create friction and which gaps are implementation limits.
whatNextRoleCannotDo: Treat reference pages as client-facing workflows
roleRelays.evidenceOrAudit: Ops changes should audit when they affect service commitments or client-visible workflow state.
roleRelays.clientVisibilityImpact: Mostly internal; improves future consistency and reduces client-facing confusion.

workflowStories.title: Tenant becomes operational
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: A family-office workspace can become active before ownership, compliance, policy, and invitation context are aligned.
handoffSequence: Invited User accepts and acknowledges scope
visibilitySequence: Internal setup visible to AlphaVest roles
Invited user sees only onboarding context
Client workspace is not treated as operational until gates are satisfied
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: A tenant is not just a record. It is the scoped collaboration container that makes later visibility and accountability possible.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: The UI is navigable with demo data; broad persisted tenant governance transactions are not fully claimed.

workflowStories.title: Client profile becomes usable shared family context
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Family information, relationships, governance roles, and objectives often sit in separate memories, emails, spreadsheets, and documents.
handoffSequence: Principal or Family CFO submits context
visibilitySequence: Analyst sees quality gaps and relationship context
Advisor sees decision-relevant context only after review
evidenceAuditSequence: Profile edits should audit when material
Missing evidence creates review tasks or data quality issues
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: The wealth map is not decoration. It is a shared model that tells every role what the family structure currently means and where uncertainty remains.
systemPrevents: advisory work based on memory alone
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Profile and relationship screens are navigable with demo data; full persistence is still a stated implementation need.

workflowStories.title: Document chaos becomes verified evidence
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Documents arrive from different people, in different formats, with uncertain sensitivity, entity links, and extracted facts.
visibilitySequence: Compliance sees evidence readiness when relevant
evidenceAuditSequence: Upload, extraction review, versioning, correction, verification, and evidence linking should be auditable
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: A document becomes useful only when it is classified, checked, linked, and made available at the correct visibility level.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: The manual documents demo UI and workflow intent; no real file storage or extraction transaction is claimed.

workflowStories.title: Signal becomes reviewed recommendation candidate
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: A signal, data gap, market prompt, or document expiry can be mistaken for advice if review boundaries are not explicit.
handoffSequence: Advisor receives a prepared candidate package
evidenceAuditSequence: Routing, request-data, and recommendation-prep actions should create traceable events
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: The system keeps signals useful without letting them masquerade as advice.
systemPrevents: AI draft or signal becoming client advice
analyst review being treated as release
workflowStories.demoBoundary: The J01 subset has demo-executable workflow actions; broad signal governance remains partial.

workflowStories.title: Advisor-approved package becomes compliance-controlled client visibility
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Advisor approval can feel final, but in a regulated wealth workflow it cannot be the same as client release.
handoffSequence: Client visibility is released or blocked with reason
visibilitySequence: Client visibility begins only after release and permission check
evidenceAuditSequence: Advisor approval, compliance release, block, and evidence request should all create audit lineage
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: Client visibility is a controlled outcome, not a side effect of internal approval.
systemPrevents: advisor approval being treated as final
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Release/block states are visible; broad release persistence remains a top implementation gap.

workflowStories.title: Client decision becomes evidence and review cadence
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: A client decision can disappear into email, meeting notes, or memory unless the decision, participants, rationale, and review date are captured together.
visibilitySequence: Advisor/compliance see outcome and evidence lineage
evidenceAuditSequence: Decision action, reason, participant action, and evidence package should be immutable/audited
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: The client decision is not the end of the workflow; it becomes the start of evidence and future review discipline.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Decision surfaces are navigable; final decision/evidence package persistence remains incomplete.

workflowStories.title: Governance change becomes permission-safe access
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Family offices frequently need to adjust access, but access changes can silently overexpose sensitive client data.
visibilitySequence: Target user sees only approved role scope
evidenceAuditSequence: Sensitive role/access actions require second confirmation and audit
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: Access is not a user setting. It is a governed collaboration boundary.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Demo actions are visible; governed access transactions remain incomplete.

workflowStories.title: Communication escalation becomes recorded service context
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Complex advisory work often shifts between digital messages, calls, workshops, and specialists without preserving why the channel changed.
handoffSequence: Compliance controls advice-like messages when needed
visibilitySequence: Client-visible messages are separated from internal notes
evidenceAuditSequence: Sensitive communication should audit; material calls can link to evidence
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: Communication is part of the workflow state, not a side channel outside the system.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Communication surfaces are mostly static; message and call persistence is not claimed.

workflowStories.title: Export request becomes redacted, approved, auditable package
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Exporting data can break the system's visibility controls if scope, redaction, approval, expiry, and audit are not explicit.
handoffSequence: Generated package becomes downloadable only if allowed
visibilitySequence: Internal evidence may become redacted package
External sharing has expiry and classification
evidenceAuditSequence: Export create, scope, redaction, approval, generation, download, and share should audit
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: An export is a controlled visibility event, not a simple download.
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Export UI is navigable; file/share realism is a Phase 18 gap.

workflowStories.title: Ops monitoring turns workflow friction into service and product improvement
workflowStories.problemBeforeAlphaVest: Workflow friction often remains anecdotal: someone knows a queue is slow, but the system does not show where or why.
handoffSequence: QA validates coverage and state language
Product identifies roadmap or implementation gaps
visibilitySequence: Client-facing promises are not inflated by reference pages
evidenceAuditSequence: Ops mutations should audit when implemented and service-affecting
workflowStories.readerTakeaway: AlphaVest uses operations feedback to keep the workflow model honest and continuously improve the shared state.
systemPrevents: reference pages being mistaken for product capability
workflowStories.demoBoundary: Queue/SLA mutation is not fully implemented; reference pages are internal only.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Defines whether a tenant is merely configured, being onboarded, operational, paused, or no longer active.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Client-side users see onboarding only after invitation; internal teams see readiness gates before activation.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Tenant create, owner assignment, policy override, activation, suspension, and archive should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: Client workspace visibility should not be treated as operational before activation gates.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Turns an untrusted file into reviewed source material or blocks it before it can support decisions.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Uploader sees upload/confirmation; analyst sees extraction/review; compliance sees evidence suitability.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Upload, extraction correction, review, block, and evidence link should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: Raw extraction and internal review are not automatically client-visible.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Generic lifecycle for action, queue, engagement, access, and service work.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Each role sees whether the next action is theirs, blocked, or waiting on someone else.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Material state transitions and sensitive actions should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: ready_for_client and client_visible are distinct from internal completion.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Separates draft/AI/internal review from advisor judgement, compliance release, and client action.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Advisor sees prepared context; compliance sees release package; client sees only released recommendation/decision material.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Approval, revision, evidence request, release, block, and client outcome should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: advisor_approved is not client_visible; only released_to_client plus permission/evidence gates allows visibility.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Controls whether sensitive/advice-like work can cross the internal-to-client boundary.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Advisor and analyst see release/block/evidence request outcome; client sees only released content.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Release, block, exception, and evidence request are audit-critical.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: released is required for advice-like client-visible content.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Tracks the client-side decision lifecycle after compliance release.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Client-side participants see required actions; advisor/compliance see outcome and evidence status.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Decision action, participant status, evidence creation, and review scheduling should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: Decision becomes visible only after release; participant scope controls who can act.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Tracks whether proof exists, is linked, validated, releasable, restricted, or obsolete.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Each role sees the proof package according to visibilityStatus and permission.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Evidence item linking, validation, release, restriction, and supersession should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: Evidence can be client-visible, redacted, restricted, or internal-only.

stateTransitionAtlas.businessMeaning: Controls how internal evidence or decision data leaves the platform as an auditable package.
stateTransitionAtlas.whatOtherRolesSee: Requesters see progress; approvers see scope/redaction; recipients see only approved package output.
stateTransitionAtlas.evidenceAuditImplications: Export scope, approval, generation, download, expiry, and revocation should audit.
stateTransitionAtlas.clientVisibilityImplications: Exports are a separate visibility surface and must preserve redaction, permission, classification, and expiry.

screenshotUsagePlan.whyTheyMatter: Show policy baseline, tenant readiness, and invitation as one activation relay.
Show client context and wealth structure becoming shared review material.
screenshotUsagePlan.handoffShown: client input -> analyst review -> advisor context

screenshotUsagePlan.whyTheyMatter: Show document inventory and evidence vault as the conversion from file to proof.
Show internal-only signal review and advisor package before release.
Show advisor approval and compliance queue as separate gates.
Show released decision and evidence lineage.
Show access and governance as controlled collaboration boundary.
Show channel escalation as service workflow state.
Use evidence/export surface to explain scoped redaction and auditable sharing.
Show ops queues as workflow feedback and improvement surface.

diagramSpecs.title: Global workflow lifecycle
diagramSpecs.source: Input --> Normalization --> Review --> Gate --> Visibility --> Decision --> Evidence --> Audit --> Monitoring
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows the universal collaboration lifecycle used by the manual.

diagramSpecs.title: Trigger to client decision swimlane
diagramSpecs.source: System signal -> Analyst route -> Advisor approval -> Compliance release/block -> Principal decision -> Evidence record
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows why advisor approval and compliance release are distinct.

diagramSpecs.title: Document intake handoff
diagramSpecs.source: empty -> uploading -> uploaded -> ai_extracted -> client_confirmed -> analyst_review_pending -> verified/needs_clarification/blocked -> linked_to_evidence
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows how files become trusted evidence or blocked work.

diagramSpecs.title: Tenant onboarding relay
diagramSpecs.source: Platform policy -> Tenant draft -> Team assignment -> Policy profile -> Principal invite -> Consent -> Role confirmation -> Tenant active readiness
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows tenant activation as a governed collaboration container.

diagramSpecs.title: Evidence and audit lineage
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows that proof is assembled from workflow events.

diagramSpecs.title: Visibility gate
diagramSpecs.source: Recommendation released_to_client + AdvisorApproval approved + ComplianceReview released + EvidenceRecord created/validated/released + Permission check true -> client_visible
diagramSpecs.purpose: Shows the no-unapproved-advice data rule in manual form.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: What AlphaVest Coordinates
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Explain data chaos, shared state, and why collaboration is the product value.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: The Collaboration Model
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Introduce Input -> Normalization -> Review -> Gate -> Visibility -> Decision -> Evidence -> Audit -> Monitoring.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Roles and Relays
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Explain how each role changes what the next role sees.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Workflow Stories
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Use the ten end-to-end stories as the main manual body.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Visibility and Client Release
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Explain internal progress versus client-visible outcomes.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Evidence, Audit, and Blocked States
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Explain proof, lineage, and protective stops as normal work.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: State Transition Atlas
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Document important lifecycle states and who can move them forward.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Screen Evidence Appendix
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Use screenshots as proof of workflow viewpoints, not as the table of contents.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Demo Boundary and Implementation Limits
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Separate navigable demo UI from production persistence claims.

pdfTocRecommendation.title: Future PDF Production Notes
pdfTocRecommendation.purpose: Guide layout, diagrams, callouts, and story-first rendering.

v3ProofWrapper.missionCard: Produce a collaboration-centered manual source package that explains workflow handoffs, state changes, and visibility gates.
evidenceIntake: manual source task and screenshot JSON
v3ProofWrapper.problemArchitecture: Current manual is too page/table/screenshot led; target manual must make cross-role collaboration and controlled visibility the primary structure.
adversarialQA: Does not make advisor approval equal client release
Does not use route paths as user instructions
v3ProofWrapper.learningLog: Future PDF production should make workflow stories the main spine and place screenshots as role-view evidence.

v2MethodArtifacts.psychoLogicMapModel: Users need to understand who is responsible next and why the system blocks premature visibility; otherwise they mistake pages for the workflow.
reframingMatrix: Field table -> state and visibility contract
v2MethodArtifacts.triz: Preserve detail without burying collaboration by moving fields into supporting contracts and making workflow stories the first-order structure.
v2MethodArtifacts.sitClosedWorld: Only existing routes, roles, states, source JSON, screenshots, and data model concepts were used.
v2MethodArtifacts.morphologicalAnalysis: Combined story chapters, role relay cards, visibility matrix, state atlas, diagram specs, and screenshot evidence plans.
scamper: Substitute route order with workflow stories
Adapt state machine language for users
Put screenshots to use as evidence
v2MethodArtifacts.harvardBatna: Good manual criteria are source-grounded, role-aware, visibility-aware, evidence-aware, and honest about demo limits; fallback is to mark unsupported behavior as a demo boundary.
measurementPlan: Ask reader who acts next after advisor approval
Ask what client sees before compliance release
Ask which evidence/audit item is created after a decision
v2MethodArtifacts.ethicsFairness: No false production claims, no invented behavior, no hidden demo limitations, no weakening of compliance or permission rules.

limitations: This is a manual source package, not final PDF design.
Some workflows are navigable demo UI but not fully persisted production transactions.
Screenshots are representative evidence, not proof of complete backend behavior.
